title: Savory Crispy Mexican Corn Huaraches
description: A crispy and flavorful Mexican dish made with corn dough, cumin seeds, and dairy-free friendly ingredients, perfect for a snack or lunch. This meal-prep friendly recipe can be made ahead of time and reheated when needed.

ingredients.name: Non-dairy cheese shreds
ingredients.notes: such as soy cheese or vegan mozzarella

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the corn masa harina, cumin seeds, and salt.
Gradually add the water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes pliable and smooth.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on the desired size of the huaraches.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten slightly into a disk shape.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry the huaraches for 2-3 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Drain the huaraches on paper towels and top with non-dairy cheese shreds, chopped onion, and chopped cilantro.
